task:
Revise the governance doc cocoder/standards/test-architecture.md to close the two gaps named in ticket 0089 (cocoder/tickets/open/0089-test-architecture-standard-adaptability-on-repo-takeover.md). This is a documentation-only edit to that ONE file. Read the ticket and the current standard first.

GAP 1 — make the rule phrasing language-neutral (today it is implicitly TypeScript/JS-shaped):
- Rule 1: '*.test.ts file' -> phrase framework-agnostically: one cohesive behavior area per test file, using whatever the repo's test-file convention is; keep '*.test.ts' only as a parenthetical example.
- Rule 2: 'a single co-located *-support.ts module' -> 'a single co-located shared-fixtures module in the repo's own language' (keep *-support.ts as the TS/JS example). The single-owner / never-copy-a-fixture rule stays.
- Rule 3: 'describe label' -> a descriptive grouping/label that names the behavior area (describe(...) as the JS example).
- Rule 4: keep the ~600-line / ~25-test soft budget as a concrete default, but state the budget is expressed in the repo's own terms (lines/tests/whatever the language idioms use); it stays a soft split-prompt, not a hard cap.
The four rules must remain four rules with the same intent — generalize wording only, do not drop or weaken any rule.

GAP 2 — strengthen the 'Existing Repos' clause from the current stub into a real detect-and-honor posture:
- On takeover, CoCoder DETECTS and HONORS the repo's established test framework, directory layout, and naming rather than rewriting to CoCoder's structure (ties to the shared standards: match surrounding style, preserve unrelated work, touch only what the task requires).
- The standard is the DEFAULT for new test work and a CONVERGENCE TARGET over time — never a forced top-down migration of a working layout.
- Where an existing layout is actively harmful (e.g. a multi-thousand-line test monolith, as CoCoder's own runner.test.ts was before run_294), CoCoder PROPOSES an opt-in split, not an imposed rewrite.
- Remove the now-stale forward-pointer sentence 'Deeper detection and honoring of a taken-over repo's conventions is tracked in ticket 0089.' since this edit IS that work. (A per-root convention-profile captured by the onboard-existing recon flow is a separate code follow-up, not part of this doc edit — do NOT touch any code or recon files.)

ACCEPTANCE: both gaps closed in cocoder/standards/test-architecture.md only; rules read framework-agnostic with TS/JS shown as examples not requirements; the Existing Repos section states detect-and-honor + default-for-new + convergence-target + propose-opt-in-split-when-harmful. MUST NOT break: keep the 'Tests never live inside [root]/cocoder/' Test Location rule, keep the Dogfooding section and the run_294 worked-example facts (5,638 lines -> 17 files + one runner.test-support.ts, 741 tests green) intact and accurate. Do not edit any other file (not the ticket, not code, not recon). Match the doc's existing terse prose style.

Create ONE new open follow-up ticket (id 0091) capturing the code-automation thread that was deliberately excluded when ticket 0089 was closed in this run. Governance-doc work only: create cocoder/tickets/open/0091-onboard-existing-capture-test-convention-profile.md AND add exactly one row to the Open table in cocoder/tickets/INDEX.md. Touch no other files; do not modify any closed-ticket rows or the 0089 entry.

Use the same frontmatter shape as the existing tickets (see cocoder/tickets/open/0087-oscar-tasking-deb.md for the exact format). Frontmatter: id: 0091; title: 'Onboard-existing recon: capture a per-root test convention profile to back the test-architecture detect-and-honor posture'; type: task; status: Open; priority: none; owner: founder-session; created: 2026-06-30.

Body sections (match the terse style of other tickets):

## Context / origin
Ticket 0089 (closed run_297) closed the two documentation gaps in cocoder/standards/test-architecture.md: the four rules are now language-neutral, and the Existing Repos clause states a detect-and-honor / default-for-new / convergence-target / propose-opt-in-split posture. But that detection is currently PROSE the persona follows by judgment during onboarding. The standard's own 'Proposed direction' floated automating it; 0089's close spun that automation out to this ticket.

## Seam
When CoCoder onboards an existing repo, nothing programmatically captures the repo's established test conventions for the standard to defer to — the persona infers framework/layout/naming each time. A captured profile would make the detect-and-honor posture repeatable instead of re-derived per session.

## Proposed direction
Extend the onboard-existing recon flow (inventoryRepo in packages/core/src/playbooks/recon.ts, surfaced via the onboard-existing path — see docs/onboarding-rebuild-ownermap.md) to capture a per-root **convention profile**: test framework, directory layout (co-located vs a tests/ dir), naming convention, and the repo's shared-fixtures module pattern. The test-architecture standard then explicitly defers to that profile. A future builder must re-verify the live recon surfaces before wiring this — the ownermap doc is a historical record.

## Founder decision pending
This would be the FIRST programmatic backing of a prose CoCoder standard (today all standards are prose followed by agents). Whether to build it, and at what scope, is a founder prioritization call — this ticket only captures the thread; it does not authorize the build.

ACCEPTANCE: the new open ticket file exists with the above content and valid frontmatter; INDEX.md Open table has one new row linking ./open/0091-onboard-existing-capture-test-convention-profile.md with title, type task, priority none, status Open. No other files changed.
